title: The Simple Blueprint to Reverse Disease

chunks.title: Introduction to the Podcast
chunks.summary: The host introduces an episode featuring a previous podcast with Dr. Paul Saladino on Heart and Soil's Radical Health Radio. He highlights Dr. Saladino's insights on detoxing from seed oils and various health topics they discussed during the episode.
chunks.transcript: on this week's episode of the podcast I wanted to repost a podcast that I did on heart and soil's radical Health radio so St is the host of this podcast he's amazing he's very eloquent and we got into a really interesting discussion that I wanted to share with my whole audience for what it's worth guys if you are not listening to radical Health radio which is like I said heart and soils podcast you're missing out Steve is an incredible host he really speaks intelligently and eloquently and they're having amazing guests so check out radical Health radio from heart and soil I'll put a link to their YouTube channel in the description here on this podcast we talked about a number of things including what I'm thinking about now what I'm excited about which is a number of things one of which is how I think you can detox from seed oils quickly I think the mainstream thinking that you have to wait four and a half years to get seed oils out of your system is wrong we talk about why there's so much confusion with seed oils we talk about what I'm changing going into 2025 St asked me about some things that I'm not a fan of in the Health Community that I would like to see go away like methylene blue there's a lot of value in this podcast and like I said I wanted to share it widely with my whole audience so enjoy this podcast on radical Health radio from hard and soil go check them out and I hope you guys enjoy this one

chunks.title: Health Momentum and Cultural Shifts
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to the momentum in the health movement, with an emphasis on how issues like seed oils are gaining attention. Dr. Saladino reflects on the cultural zeitgeist and how health conversations are finally gaining traction in mainstream media.
chunks.transcript: gentlemen this is episode 100 a huge milestone for us here at heart and soil and with radical Health radio and Paul was our first guest so naturally we had to have him on for the big one and we're recording this on New Year's Eve so it's just got all of the feels all of the specialness to it today Dr Paul how are you how was H Christmas in Costa Rica and how you feeling on the precipice of a new year is that is that kind of something you get into a lot not really but I think this year is going to be a little different I have a lot of Hope for some major change in 2025 and we'll talk about it in this podcast but yeah all is good here in Costa Rica grounding sunlight ocean keeping the tan yeah trying to avoid the blue light you know eating liver eating testicle doing all the things all the things all the good stuff yeah you touched on something I'm definitely curious to pick your brain on today which is just kind of this cultural moment that we're in right now with health having a bit of momentum you know you've been a rebel yourself a fighter for good in the ancestral Health space in the Alternative Health space if you will and it's always being this thing that's you know we feel like we're fighting a massive uphill battle and I still think it's an uphill battle that we've got in front of us with the level of metabolic illness that we face as a culture and all of that but it finally feels like there is some momentum the mar movement what does it feel like for you that's been in this fight for so long to just be on the precipice of that and be

chunks.title: Seed Oils and Their Impact
chunks.summary: Dr. Saladino delves into the concept of seed oils, discussing their historical introduction into diets and correlating them with rising chronic illness rates. He presents arguments against seed oils and outlines the issues surrounding their health implications.

chunks.title: Detoxing from Seed Oils
chunks.summary: The discussion focuses on how to detox from seed oils and the belief that it doesn't necessarily take four and a half years as commonly suggested. Two studies are referenced that support quicker detoxification, along with dietary changes for better health.
chunks.transcript: environment that we find ourselves in and we can talk about that there's a really big uphill battle that we face at every level whether it's Pharma whether it's you know Pharmacy benefit managers whether it's the insurance companies whether it's lobbying and big food and and they're all connected and they're all in bed together and that's a huge machine that we're kind of raging against at this point but there is momentum in the other direction and I think it comes from both sides right it's going to come from the top from the government maybe that's our hope now and it's gonna continue I think that when there's momentum from the top there's more momentum from the bottom there's an upwelling of people realizing when they're saying whoo the government is talking about this RFK Jr's talking about making frying oil Tallow again and Seed oils and food dyes and raw milk and it's bringing more people to an awareness that all of these issues are in the atmosphere they're in the sort of orbit and that's really cool because I think it it always goes top it always goes sort of bottom up but when it goes top down the bottom is happening too people are realizing there's more of the conversation yeah dude it's it's an incredibly hopeful time you know as we close the door on 24 and and we open up into that more hopeful future for health and these kinds of conversations at least what do you hope might we be able to say like 12 months from now looking down the barrel of 2026 like what in a more optimistic view of the world do you hope might have actually happened to food or at least the conversations around food like what what's some of Paul's big predictions for 2025 and what we might see in this space I think that there's going to be a lot of conversations about the things that we care about there's going to be a

chunks.title: Low Linoleic Acid Diet
chunks.summary: Dr. Saladino explains what a low linoleic acid diet entails, suggesting dietary restrictions on foods like chicken and eggs, which are often high in linoleic acid. He argues that this diet can lead to rapid improvements in metabolic health.

chunks.title: The Role of Mitochondrial Health
chunks.summary: The conversation transitions to the significance of mitochondrial health in relation to dietary intake. Dr. Saladino elaborates on how certain fats impact mitochondrial function and energy production within cells.

chunks.title: Light and Environmental Factors
chunks.summary: Dr. Saladino discusses the impact of light exposure and environmental factors on health. He emphasizes the importance of natural light, minimizing artificial lighting, and being mindful of non-native electromagnetic frequencies.

chunks.title: Closing Thoughts and Predictions
chunks.summary: The conversation concludes with thoughts on trends to abandon, such as methylene blue, and aims for the new year, including personal health hacks. Dr. Saladino hints at upcoming projects like a documentary on food quality.
